sources.name: Wikipedia — Contra: Hard Corps
sources.notes: 4-character system, branching paths, gameplay mechanics. North American version has health bar; Japanese version is one-hit kill.

notes: Contra: Hard Corps is the Genesis entry in the Contra series. Notably faster and more complex than original Contra.
4 PLAYABLE CHARACTERS: Sheena, Bahamut, Browny, and Ray — each with completely different weapons.
BRANCHING STORY PATHS: Multiple story branches depending on player choices at certain decision points. 5 different endings.
REGIONAL DIFFERENCE: North American version has a health bar (3 hits). Japanese/Overseas version is ONE-HIT KILL.
All characters run faster than original Contra. The game is deliberately frenetic.
Character choice creates completely different combat experiences even within the same levels.

base_movement._note: All characters share the same base movement physics but differ in weapons.

run_speed.notes: Faster than original Contra. The frenetic pace is intentional. VERIFY.

jump.notes: Full air control jump — same as original Contra.

slide.description: Slide for evasion and cover. Same mechanic as original Contra.
slide.notes: Can slide under certain projectiles and attacks.

8_directional_firing.description: All characters fire in 8 directions — same as original Contra.

characters._note: Each character's weapon set creates a completely different combat style.

ray.description: The classic Contra soldier. Standard rifle with Spread Gun, Laser, etc.
ray.weapon_set: Standard Contra weapons — spread gun, homing missiles, machine gun
ray.notes: The 'default' character for players familiar with the Contra series. Most familiar weapon behavior.

sheena.description: Female soldier. Different weapon set from Ray.
sheena.notes: Different tactical profile — players who prefer area denial.

bahamut.weapon_set: Heavy weapons — high damage per shot, slower fire rate
bahamut.notes: Slower attack rate compensated by higher damage. For aggressive close-range play.

browny.description: Tiny robot (small sprite). Fast-moving, different hitbox profile.
browny.weapon_set: Robot-specific weaponry — energy beams, burst fire

special.description: Browny's small size gives a significantly smaller hitbox — harder to hit with enemy projectiles.
special.notes: Size-based hitbox advantage is the mechanical trade-off for different weapons.

notes: Browny's small hitbox is a significant gameplay advantage — many attacks miss entirely.
The 'small character with smaller hitbox' design creates a risk-reward: master Browny's weapons or take the hitbox advantage.

characters.notes: Character selection is irreversible per run — the chosen character's weapons define the entire experience.

branching_paths._note: MAJOR ADDITION OVER PREVIOUS CONTRA GAMES. Story branches based on player choices.
branching_paths.description: At certain points in the game, the player is presented with explicit choices that lead to different stages and storylines.
branching_paths.branches: 5 possible story routes depending on choices made
branching_paths.endings: Multiple endings based on which path was taken
notes: The branching path system creates replay value — different story experiences per run.
Some choices lead to easier or harder paths through the game.
The multiple endings incentivize replaying with different characters and choices.

north_america.notes: Standard 3-hit health was standard for Western console releases.

japan_overseas.health_system: ONE HIT KILL. No health bar.
notes: The Japanese version is true to the arcade/original Contra 'pure skill' philosophy.
Dramatically harder than the NA version with the same content.
The one-hit kill version is the 'authentic' Contra experience per Konami's original design.

design_notes_for_giants_drink: Contra: Hard Corps' four-character system creates five complete games from one set of levels: each of the four characters plays differently enough that the same stage demands different strategies. Combined with branching paths, a single playthrough reveals only a fraction of the game's content.

Browny's small hitbox is a case study in how size can be a gameplay mechanic. The tiny robot's physical scale translates directly to tactical advantage — enemy projectile patterns designed for human-sized characters miss the small target. Size is a skill tool.

The Japan/North America one-hit vs three-hit regional difference reveals something about market expectations: Western audiences in 1994 expected survivability. Japanese arcade culture assumed one-hit skill mastery. Same game, profoundly different difficulty contract with the player.

The branching path system makes Contra Hard Corps a rare action game with genuine narrative agency. Most action games have a fixed path; Hard Corps lets the player shape the story through choices. The mechanics stay the same; the narrative and stage sequence changes.

The deliberately high pace of Hard Corps (faster movement, more enemies, more projectiles on screen) is a late-Genesis design philosophy: push the hardware, push the player. The game assumes mastery of the genre and sets its baseline difficulty accordingly.
